attempts.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)
    
        The image of the scene has been provided.
        Please first analyze the image and then generate the PDDL problem.
        
    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: Put a warm coffee cup into the coffee maker.
    

SCENE CAPTION:
## Scene overview
The scene is a kitchen counter area with upper cabinets, a countertop, and several appliances. The images show (1) a wooden counter with two large light-colored rectangular cutting-board-like surfaces, (2) a coffee maker on the counter, and (3) a red microwave on the counter (with cabinets above).

## Domain-relevant objects and types (with counts)
- **agent**: 1 robot agent (not visible); per problem statement it starts **not at any object location**.
- **microwave (receptacle)**: 1
  - A **red microwave** sitting on the countertop, with a front door and a numeric keypad/display on the right.
- **fridge (receptacle)**: 0 visible
- **sink (receptacle)**: 0 visible
- **knife**: 0 visible
- **other receptacles (not in domain list but may be “receptacle - object”)**:
  - **coffee maker**: 1 (acts like a receptacle/holder for a cup in the real world; visually present)
    - Beige/tan body with dark/metallic cylindrical side columns and a drip tray area.
- **coffee cup**: 0 clearly visible (no identifiable cup is shown in any of the provided views).

## Key spatial relationships (informal, mapped to possible predicate-relevant relations)
- The **coffee maker** is on the countertop near a wall/backsplash area.
- The **microwave** is also on the countertop, positioned beneath the upper cabinets.
- On the microwave counter section, a couple of small utensil-like items (spoon/fork-shaped) are visible to the right, but no domain knife is clearly identifiable.
- The two large light-colored rectangular boards/panels are on a wooden surface (likely a counter or table) in another view; they are not interacting with other domain objects.

## State information relevant to planning predicates
- **Agent location**: The agent is initially **not at any location** (must use `GotoLocation` before interacting).
- **Microwave open/closed**: The microwave door appears **closed** (no indication it is open). No clear “opened” state is visible.
- **Coffee maker open/closed/on/off**: No explicit controls/state indicators are visible; **no clear evidence** it is on, toggled, openable, or opened.
- **Warm coffee cup**:
  - A cup is **not visible**, so there is **no observable object** that can be identified as the “coffee cup,” nor any visible evidence of it being **hot/warm** (`isHot`).
- **No objects are shown being held** by the agent (`holds`, `holdsAny` are not visually instantiated).

## Visual details to distinguish main objects
- **Coffee maker**: beige/tan appliance with darker metallic-looking cylindrical sides; visible drip tray area with a patterned grate.
- **Microwave**: glossy **dark red** body, front windowed door, keypad on the right with a small greenish display.

## Summary relative to the instruction (“Put a warm coffee cup into the coffee maker.”)
- The **coffee maker is present** and can serve as the target receptacle location.
- A **coffee cup is not visible** in the provided images, so its current location and “warm/hot” state cannot be directly determined from the scene views shown.
- The **microwave is present** (potentially relevant for heating), but no cup is visible inside or near it.

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
(define (problem put_warm_coffee_cup_in_coffee_maker)
  (:domain put_task)

  (:objects
    robot - agent
    microwave1 - microwave
    coffeemaker1 - receptacle
    coffeecup1 - object
  )

  (:init
    ;; receptacle properties from the scene
    (openable microwave1)

    ;; inferred/assumed from instruction: there exists a warm (hot) coffee cup
    (isHot coffeecup1)

    ;; coffee maker is a receptacle; no evidence it is openable/opened, so leave unspecified

    ;; robot starts not holding anything
    (not (holdsAny robot))

    ;; NOTE: per prompt, robot is initially not at any object's location, so no (atLocation robot ...)
    ;; Cup location is not visible in the image; leave unspecified (not in any receptacle).
  )

  (:goal
    (and
      (inReceptacle coffeecup1 coffeemaker1)
      (isHot coffeecup1)
    )
  )
)
attempts.problem:
(define (problem put_warm_coffee_cup_in_coffee_maker)
  (:domain put_task)

  (:objects
    robot - agent
    microwave1 - microwave
    coffeemaker1 - receptacle
    coffeecup1 - object
  )

  (:init
    ;; receptacle properties from the scene
    (openable microwave1)

    ;; inferred/assumed from instruction: there exists a warm (hot) coffee cup
    (isHot coffeecup1)

    ;; coffee maker is a receptacle; no evidence it is openable/opened, so leave unspecified

    ;; robot starts not holding anything
    (not (holdsAny robot))

    ;; NOTE: per prompt, robot is initially not at any object's location, so no (atLocation robot ...)
    ;; Cup location is not visible in the image; leave unspecified (not in any receptacle).
  )

  (:goal
    (and
      (inReceptacle coffeecup1 coffeemaker1)
      (isHot coffeecup1)
    )
  )
)
attempts.plan:
(gotolocation robot coffeecup1)
(pickupobject robot coffeecup1)
(gotolocation robot coffeemaker1)
(putobjectinreceptacle robot coffeecup1 coffeemaker1)
